name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group is an AI cloud infrastructure company spun out from Yandex in 2024. Q1 2026 revenue of $399M (+684% YoY) with AI cloud revenue up 841%. FY2026 guidance: $3.0-3.4B revenue, ~40% EBITDA margin. Building large-scale GPU cloud infrastructure for AI workloads. Headquartered in Amsterdam.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Nebius transformed from Yandex's international operations into a purpose-built AI cloud platform, achieving 684% revenue growth to $399M in Q1 2026 [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): The entire business IS AI infrastructure — AI cloud revenue of $390M grew 841% YoY with 94% of revenue from AI cloud [1]. Market Share (2/2): Rapidly scaling AI cloud provider competing with hyperscalers on specialized GPU infrastructure. Product Pipeline (2/2): FY2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B implies massive continued scaling; Pennsylvania AI factory announced. R&D Intensity (0/1): Technical capabilities are strong but R&D metrics unavailable.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius's purpose of democratizing AI compute is timely and specific. Community Impact (1/2): AI infrastructure enables broader AI adoption. Employee Retention (1/2): Specialized AI talent from Yandex heritage. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue of $399M grew 684% YoY with EBITDA margin of 45% [1]. FY2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B. Profit margin of 93.1% is exceptional. ROE of 14.1% near target. D/E of 132.4% above target. Forward P/E of 643x reflects extreme growth expectations on a $59B market cap. Revenue growth is extraordinary but valuation is stretched [1].

ai_resilience.label: HIGH RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (1/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure — any shift in AI compute demand, hyperscaler competition, or technology disruption directly threatens the core business [1]. The company depends entirely on continued AI investment growth. AI Opportunity (1/1): 94% of revenue from AI cloud [1].

infinite_game.summary: Nebius shows some infinite game characteristics through its Yandex heritage and bold AI infrastructure pivot, but the company is essentially a hyper-growth startup betting entirely on AI compute demand continuation.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): Democratizing AI compute is genuine but the company exists primarily to capture AI infrastructure demand rather than serve a broader purpose [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Small, specialized team from Yandex heritage.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Competes against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) in market share terms.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The transformation from Yandex's international operations to a pure-play AI cloud company represents one of the most dramatic corporate pivots in tech [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Betting entirely on AI cloud infrastructure with massive capital deployment shows conviction [1].

investment_thesis: Nebius is one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure companies globally, with Q1 2026 revenue of $399M (+684% YoY) and AI cloud revenue growing 841% with 45% EBITDA margin [1]. FY2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B implies 7-8x annual growth. The Yandex heritage provides deep AI/ML technical capabilities, and the Pennsylvania AI factory announcement signals continued infrastructure scaling.
thesis_continued: The fundamental risk is extreme valuation — $59B market cap on a company that generated $399M in Q1 and guided to $3.0-3.4B for FY2026. Forward P/E of 643x and D/E of 132% reflect massive growth expectations [1]. Any slowdown in AI infrastructure spending, hyperscaler competition (AWS, Azure, GCP building own capacity), or technology shifts away from GPU compute could devastate the thesis. The Yandex/Russia heritage creates geopolitical perception risk.
key_catalysts: FY2026 revenue guidance of $3.0-3.4B representing ~8x annual growth from the AI cloud scaling trajectory [1]
Pennsylvania AI factory and continued GPU infrastructure buildout expanding compute capacity
45% EBITDA margin demonstrating AI cloud unit economics at scale
key_risks: Extreme valuation at 643x forward P/E with $59B market cap on $1.6B annualized revenue [1]
Hyperscaler competition — AWS, Azure, GCP have vastly more capital and existing customer relationships
Single-sector dependency on AI compute demand — any macro pullback in AI investment would directly impact revenue
sources: Nebius Q1 2026 Earnings — Yahoo Finance, Futurum Group, Tickeron, 2026
Yahoo Finance — NBIS Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
